steps.name: Lower the deterministic variable-text forms and narrow the unsupported gate to shuffle-only
steps.description: Land the variable-text lowering mechanism (ADR-010) and flip matrix rows 25-27 from reject to compile, GREENing the VT1 once-only acceptance scenarios (vt-once-sticky, vt-once-bare). Implement: (1) ContentSegment.variableText(mode:stages:) AST case + VariableTextMode enum {sequence,cycle,once}; (2) a variable-text parse rule in InkParser.scanBraceSegment recognising a brace body with a top-level '|' and no top-level ':', reading the leading marker (&->cycle, !->once, none->sequence), stripping it, and '|'-splitting the body into stages; (3) a new stateless VariableTextEmitter (Compiler/Codegen/) with ONE parametrized routine over (op, bound, appendEmptyStage) emitting the #f:5 dispatch container + named seqN-sI stage containers + shared seqN-end rejoin, mirroring ConditionalEmitter's named-container/-end-rejoin pattern; (4) a .variableText dispatch branch in RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerBody parallel to the inline-conditional branch, threading line suffix + rest-of-body as the continuation; (5) narrow UnsupportedConstructDetector.variableTextConstruct to return a construct ONLY for the '~' shuffle marker (sequence/cycle/once return nil), preserving the inline-conditional ':' discriminator and all statement-level rejects. Then reconcile the two existing reject suites that RED on the gate change and the feature-reference prose. Per DISTILL upstream-issues U-2 (authoritative) and DESIGN DDD-5, the gate flips to shuffle-only in this slice, so sequence and cycle ALSO compile here; their BOUNDARY verification scenarios (clamp-at-last, modulo-wrap, mixed) remain slices 02/03 and stay .disabled.
acceptance_criteria: VT1: vt-once-sticky compiles natively and plays ['The lock clicks open.'] (emitted exactly once), line-for-line identical to the committed inklecate oracle
VT1: vt-once-bare compiles natively and plays ['The corridor falls quiet.'] (bare form lowers as a plain sequence with a leading empty stage; text emits on the second visit), identical to the oracle
VT0 shuffle regression guard stays GREEN: a '{~a|b}' shuffle still rejects with a located, construct-named .unsupportedConstruct error and produces no story
S6 UnsupportedRejection: reject-seq, reject-cycle, reject-once are removed from rejectCorpus; reject-shuffle plus the statement-level constructs (thread/list/random/external) still reject

S5 FeatureReferenceConsistency: the three variable-text rows move from documentedUnsupported to documentedSupported (point them at the vt-once-sticky / vt-seq-three / vt-cycle-two family, which carry committed oracles and compile); shuffle/thread/list/random/external stay documentedUnsupported
docs/product/ink-feature-reference.md rows 25-27 change MUST-REJECT -> MUST-COMPILE and the surrounding prose is updated to match (S5 suite is the SSOT the prose mirrors)
Both VT1 ATs have their .disabled trait removed (zero .disabled VT1 ATs remain for slice 01)
The full pre-existing suite stays GREEN (288 baseline at DISTILL hand-off, now +2 enabled VT1 scenarios); no regression in any Compiler_S* suite
Compiler-only diff: no change under Engine/, Decoder/, or Facade/; SwiftLint --strict R1/R3/R5 boundary rules pass (Compiler/ imports no Engine/, calls no JSONSerialization)
steps.criteria: Both VT1 OnceOnly ATs pass GREEN with their .disabled removed; VT0 shuffle guard, S5 and S6 suites pass GREEN after their slice-01 edits; the full swift test suite is GREEN; SwiftLint --strict passes; no Engine/Decoder/Facade file is modified.
steps.scenario_name: US-01 — Compile and play a once-only variable-text form ({!a|b} / {|x|})
quality_gates: Both VT1 OnceOnly acceptance tests pass (native == oracle)
VT0 shuffle reject guard remains GREEN
Compiler_S5 and Compiler_S6 suites pass after their reject->supported reconciliation
Full swift test suite GREEN (no regressions)
implementation_notes: Lowering spec (DESIGN ground truth, verified vs inklecate): per form (OP, BOUND, append-empty): sequence = (MIN, S-1, no); cycle = (%, S, no); once = (MIN, S [new last index after appending], yes-1). Dispatch body: `ev visit BOUND OP /ev`, then per stage `ev du I == /ev {"->":"seqN-sI","c":true}`, then `nop`. Stage container seqN-sI = [pop, <^text if non-empty>, {"->":"seqN-end"}]; an empty stage omits the ^text node. seqN-end holds the continuation (line suffix + rest-of-body).

Worked examples: {!The lock clicks open.|} -> strip '!' -> '|'-split ['The lock clicks open.', ''] -> append empty -> ['The lock clicks open.','',''], visit 2 MIN (emits stage0 on visit0, blank after). Bare {|The corridor falls quiet.|} -> no marker -> sequence -> '|'-split ['','The corridor falls quiet.',''], visit 2 MIN (emits on visit1).

Mirror ConditionalEmitter exactly for boundary mechanics: nextOrdinal-style stable keys, key()/path() helpers, BranchLowerer/ExpressionLowerer typealiases, registering containers into the inout named collector, #f flag = 5 on the dispatch container (Visits | CountStartOnly so `visit` yields the 0-based own read count).

Use a DISTINCT key prefix (seq{N}-*) from the conditional prefix (cond{N}-*) so a body mixing variable-text and conditionals cannot collide (DESIGN OQ-3, proven by construction; verified by the vt-mixed scenario in slice 02).

In lowerBody, detect a .variableText segment in a .content line parallel to the existing inline-conditional detection (firstIndex(where:)); reuse inlineContinuationStatements + branchLowerer for the continuation, exactly like lowerInlineConditionalLine.

Gate: in UnsupportedConstructDetector, leadingMarker maps ONLY '~' -> 'shuffle'; '&' and '!' now return nil; the no-marker top-level-'|' path returns nil (was 'sequence'). Keep checkStatement (thread/list/random/external) and the conditional ':' discriminator untouched. The shuffle path must still throw a located, NAMED error (VT0 + S6 depend on it).

S5 reconciliation: reuse the existing committed vt-* oracle fixtures for documentedSupported rather than re-pointing the escaped reject-once (which is a latent \! sequence per U-3); the S5 supported test only compiles + builds Story (no oracle play), so any compilable variable-text fixture satisfies it.

Per ADR-025 trunk-based: RED (enable VT1, watch it fail for MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY per distill/red-classification.md) -> GREEN -> COMMIT directly to main. The pre-commit gate (.githooks/pre-commit: swiftlint --strict + swift test) must pass for the commit; do NOT --no-verify.

Confirm genuine RED before GREEN: with the gate unchanged and VT1 enabled, compileAndPlay throws CompileError (.unsupportedConstruct) — the correct MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY red, not a fixture/setup defect.
